question.text: PAN serves as the unique identification number for all participants transacting in the securities market. This requirement applies:

answer.text: Irrespective of the amount of transaction
Only for transactions above Rs. 2 lakh
Only for transactions in the derivatives segment

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** PAN is the unique identification number for ALL participants transacting in the securities market, IRRESPECTIVE of the amount of transaction. There is no minimum threshold for PAN requirement in the securities market.
**The Trap:** Unlike income tax provisions where PAN is required above certain thresholds, in the securities market PAN is mandatory for ALL transactions regardless of amount. Even a Rs. 100 investment requires PAN.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This is from the workbook sample questions. In NISM V-A, PAN is mandatory for mutual fund investments (except SIP up to Rs. 50,000/year). In NISM III-A, PAN is mandatory for all broking transactions.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always verify PAN online at the Income Tax website. Do not insist on the original or copy of PAN card -- online verification is sufficient. Also check that Aadhaar-PAN linkage is active, as an unlinked PAN becomes inoperative.
